text:
happen add or subtract
some minutes from lagna longitude (i.e correct the time)
accordingly so that Kunda longitude falls on Nakshatra (Birth
star) of native. Once it matches, same should be taken as
correct lagna longitude (and the corresponding query time as
correct query time)] ·
Author of Prasnamarga lived in 16th century, when instead
of watches length of shadow of gnomon was used to calculate
time. Therefore use of such methods was a must in that period
to verify the accuracy of time, so that it could be ensured that
query moment is in rhythm with native's destiny.
Here is an inherent conflict within the sloka, which needs
to be understood. Prasnamarga states that,
longitude of Asc x 81 =Longitude of Birth Star of native

gB~ir~th~~~i~m~e~R~e~ct~if~ic~a~tio~n~M~et~h~od~s~~-------------------49
Note that the reference is not to Longitude of Moon at
query time as we may expect. This indicates that
Prasnamargacharya was applying a method that was regularly
